steps: verify WSL, the selected distribution, tmux, and WezTerm CLI
allocate the smallest unused positive instance ID
reserve the bare dev session for open-or-activate
create exactly one detached tmux session named dev-<id>
construct one unique WezTerm workspace and window class
invoke wezterm start --always-new-process with an explicit WSL tmux attach command
emit a local launch plan and launch result
require a separate end-to-end lane for visible-window and attached-client proof
successConditions: the session name was absent before creation
the workspace and window class are unique to the session
the launch result does not claim visible-window observation
failureRules: existing explicit instance ID blocks rather than attaches
missing WSL, tmux, or WezTerm blocks
session creation failure blocks WezTerm launch
one click may not attach a second window to dev
process acknowledgement cannot be promoted to end-to-end runtime proof
proofCeiling: Session creation and separate-process command acknowledgement until the exact desktop shortcut is observed end to end.
